# Social influence bias: a randomized experiment

**Authors:** Lev Muchnik, Sinan Aral, Sean J. Taylor  
**Year:** 2013  
**URL:** https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/23929980/  
**Evidence type:** Randomized field experiment

## Claim supported

Artificial initial positive social feedback can causally increase later positive ratings and final score.

## Key result

Positive manipulation increased likelihood of positive ratings by 32% and final ratings by 25% on average.

## How it supports manufactured traction

This source strengthens the argument that digital systems can be moved by manipulated proxy signals. Use it as evidence for the mechanism, not as proof that every campaign succeeds.
